nodes.label: Economics
nodes.description: My core discipline — how individuals, firms, and governments allocate scarce resources. B.S. at Cal Poly.

nodes.label: Real Estate
nodes.description: Property markets, valuation, development, and investment. Real Property Development minor at Cal Poly.

nodes.label: Construction Management
nodes.description: Materials, scheduling, cost control, and project delivery. Starting at Cumming Group August 2026.

nodes.label: Sustainability
nodes.description: Green building, resource efficiency, and long-term environmental thinking. Central to my senior research.

nodes.label: Data & Analysis
nodes.description: Turning raw numbers into meaning — statistics, programming, and applied research.

nodes.label: Business & Finance
nodes.description: Strategy, corporate finance, entrepreneurship, and organizational leadership.

nodes.label: The Ocean
nodes.description: Surfing, the coast, and everything that comes with a life near the water.

nodes.label: Philosophy
nodes.description: Ethics, existentialism, and eastern thought — explored through UCSC coursework and personal reading.

nodes.label: Econometrics
nodes.description: Statistical identification strategies for causal inference. Applied OLS, DiD, CEM, and event studies in my senior project (ECON 339, 460).

nodes.label: International Trade
nodes.description: Theory of trade flows, comparative advantage, and global markets (ECON 404).

nodes.label: Urban Economics
nodes.description: How cities form, grow, and distribute resources. Closely linked to real estate and development.

nodes.label: Labor & Inequality
nodes.description: Economics of poverty, discrimination, and immigration (ECON 303).

nodes.label: Real Estate Finance
nodes.description: Financing structures, valuation, and investment analysis for real property (BUS 434).

nodes.label: Property Development
nodes.description: Principles of land use, entitlement, and real property development (CM 475).

nodes.label: Property Valuation
nodes.description: How certifications, renovations, and market factors drive assessed value — the core of my senior project.

nodes.label: Land & Water Econ
nodes.description: Economics of natural resources, land markets, and water rights (ECON 435).

nodes.label: Cost Management
nodes.description: Quantity takeoffs, budget estimates, change order review, and cost analysis. My role at Cumming Group (CM 232).

nodes.label: Materials & Assemblies
nodes.description: Properties and performance of building materials and assemblies (CM 113).

nodes.label: Project Planning
nodes.description: Scheduling, logistics, and managing complex construction projects (CM 371).

nodes.label: LEED & Green Building
nodes.description: LEED and Energy Star certification systems. Research shows certified SF buildings carry a 31–52% assessed value premium.

nodes.label: Environmental Economics
nodes.description: How markets handle environmental goods and externalities (ECON 431, 435).

nodes.label: Climate Change
nodes.description: Global climate systems, policy responses, and resource implications (NR 310).

nodes.label: Environmental Planning
nodes.description: Land use planning with an environmental lens (CRP 336).

nodes.label: Python
nodes.description: Primary analysis language. Used for data cleaning, regression, CEM matching, and all figures in my senior project (CSE 20).

nodes.label: Statistics
nodes.description: Probability theory and statistical inference — foundation for all empirical work (STAT 131, 301).

nodes.label: R
nodes.description: Statistical computing language. Want to explore alongside Python for econometric research.

nodes.label: Corporate Finance
nodes.description: Capital structure, valuation, and financial decision-making (BUS 342, 439).

nodes.label: Business Strategy
nodes.description: Competitive dynamics and strategic decision-making (ECON 136, BUS 310).

nodes.label: Entrepreneurship
nodes.description: Starting and growing ventures. Exploring the builder mindset (BUS 310).

nodes.label: Leadership
nodes.description: How organizations and people are led effectively (BUS 382).

nodes.label: Board Shaping
nodes.description: I shape my own surfboards — 5 so far. Fish, quads, and experimental designs.

nodes.label: Coastal Ecology
nodes.description: How coastlines change and how human activity shapes coastal systems (EART 106).

nodes.label: Ethics & Technology
nodes.description: The moral dimensions of science and technology (PHIL 323).

nodes.label: Eastern Philosophy
nodes.description: Taoism, Buddhism, and non-Western ways of thinking. Read the Tao Te Ching and Buddhist texts at UCSC.
